channel.title: Projects on tom79
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on tom79

item.title: Fedilab
item.description: Fedilab lets you access multiple Fediverse accounts from a single Android app. Whether you use Mastodon, Pleroma, Pixelfed, PeerTube, or Misskey, you can manage all your accounts in one place without switching between apps. Supported platforms Platform Support Mastodon Full Pleroma / Akkoma Full, with reactions Misskey Full PeerTube Dedicated video module Pixelfed Via Mastodon API Lemmy Public timelines GNU Social Public timelines Key features Multi-account: manage accounts across different instances and platforms Timelines: Home, Local, Public, Hashtag, and Trending Post editor: compose with media, polls, content warnings, and scheduling Conversations: direct messages with threaded view Cross-posting: share content across your accounts in one action Thread tracking: highlights new replies since your last visit Built-in browser: open links without leaving the app Translation: translate posts directly in the timeline Content filters: fine-grained keyword filtering per context Scheduled posts: write now, publish later Themes: Light, Dark, Black, Solarized, Dracula, and dynamic colors on Android 12+ Accessibility: mandatory alt text option for media uploads Privacy-friendly notifications: UnifiedPush support on F-Droid Proxy support: configure custom network settings

item.title: Holos App
item.description: Holos App is a mobile Fediverse platform where your phone IS your ActivityPub server. Unlike traditional clients, Holos runs a complete decentralized server directly on your device. You own your keys, your data, and your identity. How it works Holos embeds a Node.js server on the device that speaks native ActivityPub. A relay server provides a stable identity (@you@relay.domain) and proxies requests to your phone, even when your IP changes. When you’re offline, activities are queued and delivered when you reconnect.

item.title: Holos Relay Server
item.description: The Holos Relay Server solves a fundamental problem: mobile devices cannot have stable URLs. The relay provides permanent identities like @alice@relay.domain and proxies ActivityPub requests to the user’s phone, wherever it is. How it works When a Fediverse server wants to reach a Holos user, it contacts the relay at their stable address. The relay forwards the request to the phone through a WebSocket tunnel. When the phone is offline, activities are queued and delivered on reconnect.

item.title: Holos Discover
item.description: Holos Discover is a search engine for the Fediverse that only indexes content from users who have explicitly opted in. It works as a native ActivityPub participant, not a web crawler. Users keep full control over their data and can opt out at any time. How it works An admin seeds initial users via CLI or subscribes to ActivityPub relays Holos Discover sends Follow requests to users who meet the consent criteria When accepted, their public posts are indexed for full-text search New users are discovered organically from mentions, boosts, and replies The network grows naturally while always respecting user privacy Consent model A user is only indexed if all of these are true:

item.title: PawFed
item.description: PawFed is a collaborative federated map for animal welfare. The map starts empty and is populated by Fediverse users who mention the ActivityPub actor (@pawfed@pawfed.org) from their usual client (Mastodon, Misskey, etc.) with anything that can help an animal: shelters, food donations, foster families, reports, solidarity vets, water points, and more. PawFed has its own ActivityPub actor (type Service) that federates content. Points on the map are published as AP Notes, can receive likes and boosts from the Fediverse, and the actor can be followed by other instances.

item.title: UntrackMe
item.description: UntrackMe intercepts links on your Android device and redirects them to privacy-friendly front-ends. It also strips tracking parameters from any URL, giving you cleaner, tracker-free browsing. It works system-wide and transparently, no manual copy-paste needed. Supported redirections Platform Redirects to Twitter / X Nitter YouTube Invidious Instagram Bibliogram Reddit Teddit Medium Scribe Wikipedia Wikiless TikTok ProxiTok Google Maps OpenStreetMap Key features URL cleaning: removes tracking parameters (UTM, Google Analytics, Facebook, and more) via dynamic rules Short URL unrolling: follows redirections from t.

item.title: CastLab
item.description: CastLab lets you stream photos, videos, and music from your phone to any DLNA/UPnP or FCast compatible device on your local network. Browse your gallery, build playlists, extract videos from the web, and cast everything to your TV or speakers. Supported devices CastLab works with any device that supports DLNA/UPnP or FCast, including smart TVs, media players, Chromecast alternatives, and network speakers. Devices are discovered automatically on your local network.

item.title: FediPlan
item.description: FediPlan is a web application to schedule posts on Mastodon and Pleroma. Log in with your Fediverse account, compose your message, pick a date and time, and FediPlan publishes it for you. You can use it on the official instance or host your own. A mobile version is also available: FediPlan App. Key features Schedule posts: pick a date and time, FediPlan handles the rest Multi-account: manage and schedule across multiple Fediverse accounts Post editor: compose with media attachments, polls, content warnings, and visibility settings Manage scheduled posts: view, edit, and cancel upcoming publications Mastodon and Pleroma: compatible with both platforms via OAuth Self-hostable: deploy your own instance with Docker or a manual PHP setup Translations: community-translated via Crowdin in 25+ languages

item.title: FediPlan App
item.description: FediPlan App is the mobile companion to FediPlan. Schedule posts on Mastodon and Pleroma directly from your phone. Pick a date and time, compose your message, and FediPlan App takes care of publishing it for you. Key features Multi-account: schedule posts across all your Fediverse accounts Date and time picker: calendar and clock interface to choose when to publish Post editor: full compose screen with character counter respecting instance limits Media support: attach images with cropping, alt text descriptions, and sensitive media toggle Polls: create polls with multiple options Content warnings: add spoiler text to your scheduled posts Emoji picker: standard and custom instance emoji Visibility control: public, unlisted, private, or direct Language selection: tag your posts with the right language Manage scheduled posts: view, edit, and delete upcoming posts Pleroma support: compatible with Pleroma-specific features like content formats Themes: Light, Dark, and Auto Translations: available in English, French, German, Russian, Turkish, and Chinese
